prompt_text: Best CRM that works with Gmail and Mailchimp
response:
The **strongest all‑round options** that work well with both **Gmail and Mailchimp** are **HubSpot CRM, Copper, Capsule, and Zoho CRM**; if you want something lighter or more automated, tools like **folk CRM**, **Coffee**, or **Method:CRM** are also worth a look.[1][3][5][6][7]

Below is a concise comparison to help you choose, based on Gmail and Mailchimp integration quality plus overall usability.

| CRM | Gmail integration | Mailchimp integration | Best for |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **HubSpot CRM** | Native Gmail add‑on, email tracking & logging inside inbox (widely documented) | Sync contacts, use Mailchimp lists with HubSpot data via native/connector apps | Teams wanting a robust free plan with strong sales & marketing features |
| **Copper** | Built specifically for **Google Workspace**; lives in Gmail sidebar, auto‑logs email & calendar[6] | Copper itself highlights it “makes a perfect pair” with Mailchimp via direct integration[6] | Google‑centric companies that live in Gmail and want minimal setup |
| **Capsule CRM** | Integrates smoothly with **Gmail**; can capture emails and use Google Workspace apps[5] | Direct Capsule–Mailchimp integration for list sync and segmenting[5] | Small businesses needing a simple, clean CRM with Mailchimp sync |
| **Zoho CRM** | Gmail integration and G Suite connectors (email sync, calendar) (general Zoho docs) | Mailchimp integration via native connector/Zoho Flow; widely used together[1] | Price‑sensitive teams wanting lots of features and customization |
| **folk CRM** | Designed around email workflows; integrates with Gmail for contact/engagement tracking[1] | Ranked as **“best CRM for Mailchimp users”** in a 2026 roundup[1] | Lightweight, modern CRM where Mailchimp is central to marketing |
| **Coffee (AI agent CRM)** | Connects to Google Workspace via tools like Zapier; automates workflows[3] | Uses Zapier to connect to Mailchimp and Google tools, automating 8–12 hours/week[3] | Teams wanting AI automation and are comfortable with Zapier-style setups |
| **Method:CRM** | Integrations with Google apps (email/calendar) via connectors (Method docs) | Purpose‑built **Mailchimp CRM integration** to drive campaigns from CRM data[7] | Service businesses, especially QuickBooks users, needing Mailchimp sync |

### How to pick the “best” for your use case

- **If you live in Gmail all day**  
  - **Copper** is the most Gmail‑native: it runs inside Gmail and is marketed as a Google‑first CRM.[6]  
  - **Capsule** is also praised for smooth Gmail integration and simplicity.[5]

- **If Mailchimp is your main marketing engine**  
  - **folk CRM** is explicitly positioned as the **best CRM for Mailchimp users** in a 2026 comparison, focusing on keeping contact data clean and turning engagement into next steps.[1]  
  - **Capsule** has a mature, direct Mailchimp integration and is recommended specifically as a “best CRM for Mailchimp.”[5]  
  - **Method:CRM** markets itself directly as “CRM software integrated with Mailchimp,” aimed at simplifying campaign sending from CRM data.[7]

- **If you want automation and AI on top of Gmail + Mailchimp**  
  - **Coffee** is an AI agent CRM that connects to Mailchimp and Google tools via Zapier, aiming to automate 8–12 hours of weekly manual work.[3]  
  - **HubSpot** plus a Mailchimp connector can give you more advanced workflows, reporting, and sales/marketing automation.

- **If budget and flexibility matter most**  
  - **HubSpot CRM** has a generous free tier and good Gmail integration, plus connectors for Mailchimp.  
  - **Zoho CRM** is usually cheaper at scale and offers extensive customization, with integrations to both Gmail and Mailchimp.[1]

If you tell me your team size, budget (e.g., “must be free” vs “OK up to $X/user”), and whether sales or marketing is more important, I can narrow this down to 1–2 very specific recommendations.
